info.title: PayID Platform API
info.description: API documentation for the PayID Platform — including OAuth 2.0 Authentication, Organization Management, API Keys, KYC Verification, Analytics, Billing, and more.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: OAuth login, registration, and session management

tags.name: OAuth 2.0
tags.description: OAuth 2.0 standard endpoints (authorize, token, revoke, introspect)

tags.name: OAuth Clients
tags.description: Register and configure OAuth client applications

tags.name: API Keys
tags.description: Create and manage programmatic API keys

tags.name: Analytics
tags.description: Usage, MAU, error rate, and latency metrics

tags.name: Billing
tags.description: Manage billing information and subscription tier

get.description: Returns OAuth token issuance, active token, and overall API usage metrics for the organization.

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description: Filter metrics by specific OAuth client

get.description: Returns Monthly Active Users (MAU) statistics for the organization.

parameters.name: month
parameters.description: Month to query in ISO 8601 format (default is current month)

get.description: Returns error rate and distribution analytics for the organization.
Returns API latency percentile (p50, p95, p99) metrics for the organization.

post.description: Creates a new API key for the organization. The full key value is returned only once in this response — store it securely as it cannot be retrieved again.

expiresAt.description: Optional expiry date in ISO 8601 format

201.description: API key created — includes the full key value (only shown once)

get.description: Returns all API keys for the organization, excluding the actual key values.
Returns metadata for a specific API key (key value is never returned after creation).

delete.description: Permanently revokes an API key. Revoked keys cannot be re-enabled.

get.description: Returns a paginated, filterable list of audit log events for the organization. Logs are immutable.

parameters.name: actorId
parameters.description: Filter by the user ID who performed the action

get.description: Returns a single audit log entry by ID.

post.description: Authenticates a user with email and password. Returns a session cookie or token depending on `return_token`.

return_url.description: URL to redirect to after login

return_token.description: If true, returns a JWT token instead of setting a session cookie

post.description: Creates a new user account and logs them in.

400.description: Validation error or email already exists

post.description: Sends a one-time magic link to the user's email address for passwordless login.

shouldCreate.description: If true, creates the user account if not found

get.description: Verifies a magic link token and creates a session or returns a JWT.

200.description: Token verified, session created or JWT returned

post.description: Sends a one-time passcode to the user's email address.

shouldCreate.description: If true, creates the user account if not found

post.description: Verifies an OTP sent via email and creates a session or returns a JWT.

200.description: OTP verified, session created or JWT returned

get.description: Redirects the user to Google's OAuth 2.0 authorization page.
Handles the callback from Google after user authorization. Creates a session or returns a JWT.

302.description: Redirect to return_url or app dashboard

get.description: Checks whether the current request has a valid OAuth session via cookie.

post.description: Destroys the current session cookie and logs the user out.

get.description: Initiates the OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow. Redirects the user to the consent/login screen. Supports PKCE (`code_challenge` + `code_challenge_method`).

parameters.name: code_challenge
parameters.description: PKCE code challenge (Base64URL-encoded SHA-256 hash of code_verifier)

302.description: Redirects to consent page or redirect_uri with authorization code

post.description: Handles the user's consent decision (approve or deny) from the consent form.

302.description: Redirects to redirect_uri with code (allow) or error (deny)

get.description: Returns the current billing information and subscription tier for the organization.

patch.description: Updates billing email or payment method. Requires `billing:update` permission.

post.description: Upgrades the organization's subscription tier.

400.description: Already on requested tier or higher

post.description: Downgrades the organization's subscription tier.

get.description: Returns public-facing details about an OAuth client. Used for displaying consent screens. No authentication required.

200.description: Public client information (name, logo, allowed scopes)

post.description: Registers a new OAuth 2.0 client application for the organization.

201.description: OAuth client created (includes client_secret, shown only once)

get.description: Returns a paginated list of all OAuth clients registered for the organization.
Returns full configuration details for a specific OAuth client.

patch.description: Updates OAuth client configuration such as redirect URIs, scopes, grant types, branding, and token settings.

delete.description: Permanently deletes an OAuth client. All active tokens issued under this client will be revoked.

post.description: Generates a new client secret and invalidates the old one. The new secret is returned only once.

get.description: Returns token issuance counts and usage statistics for a specific OAuth client.

post.description: Creates an additional secret for the OAuth client. Useful for secret rotation with zero downtime.

201.description: Secret created (full value shown only once)

get.description: Returns metadata for all secrets associated with the OAuth client (secret values are not returned).

patch.description: Updates a secret's status (enable/disable) or expiration date.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a client secret. The secret must be disabled before deletion.

400.description: Secret must be disabled before deletion

post.description: Validates and returns metadata about an access token or refresh token. Used by resource servers to verify tokens. Requires client authentication.
Starts a KYC identity verification session using the specified provider (QoreID or Plaid). Supports BVN, NIN, passport, and various international ID types.

idNumberType.description: International ID type (e.g. ng_nin, us_ssn, za_smart_id)

get.description: Returns the current status of a KYC verification session.

parameters.name: refresh
parameters.description: If true, fetches the latest status from the provider

post.description: Retries a failed or expired KYC verification session using the specified strategy.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of KYC verification sessions, with optional filtering by provider, status, and date range.

post.description: Receives and processes webhook events from Plaid Identity Verification. Signature validated using Plaid's webhook signature header.
Receives and processes webhook events from QoreID Identity Verification. Validates authenticity using QoreID's signature header.

get.description: Returns the KYC provider configuration for an organization, including enabled providers and webhook settings.

patch.description: Updates the KYC provider configuration, including enabling/disabling providers, priority order, and webhook settings.

get.description: Returns all KYC verification sessions for users under an organization, with filtering and pagination.

200.description: Paginated KYC verifications for the organization

get.description: Returns KYC verification success rates, provider breakdown, and trend analytics for the organization.

post.description: Sends an email invitation to join the organization. Requires `member:invite` permission.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of organization members, optionally filtered by role.

patch.description: Changes a member's role within the organization. Requires `member:update` permission.

delete.description: Removes a member from the organization. Requires `member:remove` permission.

post.description: Accepts a pending organization invitation using the token from the invitation email.

200.description: Invitation accepted, user added to organization

post.description: Declines a pending organization invitation.

get.description: Returns details about an invitation (organization name, inviter, role). No authentication required.

post.description: Creates a new organization. The authenticated user becomes the owner.

get.description: Lists all organizations the authenticated user is a member of.
Returns full details for a specific organization.

patch.description: Updates organization name, settings, or billing details. Requires `org:update` permission.

delete.description: Permanently deletes an organization. Requires owner role.

403.description: Only owner can delete the organization

post.description: Transfers organization ownership to another member. Requires owner role.

newOwnerId.description: User ID of the new owner (must be an existing member)

403.description: Only the current owner can transfer ownership

post.description: Suspends an organization, disabling access for all members. Requires `org:update` permission or platform admin.
Reactivates a previously suspended organization. Requires `org:update` permission or platform admin.

get.description: Exports all organization data for GDPR/data portability. Requires `org:read` permission.

post.description: Revokes an access token or refresh token. Revoking a refresh token will also invalidate all associated access tokens.

token.description: The access or refresh token to revoke

get.description: Returns security, token TTL, rate limiting, and webhook settings for the organization.

patch.description: Updates security, token TTL, rate limiting, and webhook settings. Requires `org:update` permission.

allowedDomains.description: Allowed email domains for user sign-up

rotateWebhookSecret.description: If true, rotates the webhook signing secret

post.description: Issues access tokens, ID tokens, and refresh tokens. Supports grant types: `authorization_code`, `refresh_token`, and `client_credentials`. Authenticate using HTTP Basic authentication with `client_id:client_secret` or include credentials in the request body.

code_verifier.description: PKCE code verifier (required if code_challenge was used)

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's profile.

patch.description: Updates the authenticated user's profile fields.

get.description: Looks up a user by their unique PayTag identifier.
Returns KYC verification records for the current authenticated user.
Returns KYC records for a specific user. Users can only access their own records.

403.description: Cannot access another user's KYC records

get.description: Returns all OAuth consent grants for the current authenticated user.
Returns a specific OAuth consent grant by ID.

delete.description: Revokes an existing OAuth consent grant. Any tokens issued under this consent will also be revoked.

get.description: Returns claims about the authenticated user based on the provided access token. The claims returned depend on the scopes granted during authorization.
Returns the OpenID Connect discovery document, which describes all supported endpoints, grant types, scopes, and signing algorithms.
Returns the public keys used to sign JWT access tokens and ID tokens. Resource servers use this endpoint to verify token signatures.
